channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pete Buttigieg will announce he won't seek US Senate seat in Michigan, source says
item.description: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg is planning to announce that he won't seek Michigan's open U.S. Senate seat in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How an anti-evolution law a century ago set up an infamous showdown over religion in public schools
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — They called it the “monkey trial.” It was supposed to be a publicity stunt. A hundred years later, it is remembered as far more. In March 1925, Tennessee became the first state in the country to ban the teaching of evolution in public school classrooms.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices, but the US is hesitant
item.description: Without a new policy including vaccines, the government will continue to slaughter every flock with a bird flu infection to limit the spread of the disease. Those deaths have totaled over 166 million birds in the U.S. since 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-Texas megachurch pastor indicted in Oklahoma on child sex abuse charges
item.description: A former pastor of a Texas megachurch who resigned after a woman accused him of sexually abusing her in the 1980s has been indicted in Oklahoma on child sexual abuse charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missing American college student search potentially complicated by power outage: expert
item.description: A power outage at the Punta Cana, Dominican Republic, resort where a missing American college student had been vacationing could potentially impact the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawsuit claims Girl Scout cookies contain 'heavy metals,' pesticides
item.description: A new federal lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain heavy metals, but the organization assures the cookies are safe.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KTLA 5

item.title: Immigrants fuel growth in major US urban counties
item.description: Immigrants kept the largest urban counties in the U.S. growing last year. Core counties in the Houston, Miami and Phoenix metropolitan areas grew more than any others in the country primarily because of people moving in from outside the United States, according to population estimates from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration resumes detention of immigrant families after Biden-era pause
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration resumed family detention of immigrants last week in a South Texas facility after a Biden-era pause, a legal nonprofit group providing services to migrant families said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Child illegally taken by her mother from US to Mexico in 1999 is found and reconnects with dad
item.description: Carlos Reyes-Couvertier spent the past quarter century trying to find his missing daughter. There were multiple trips to Mexico, where police believed she was living after having been abducted by her mother.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about US student Sudiksha Konanki, who vanished in Dominican Republic on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Investigators continue to search for a college student from Virginia who vanished earlier this month while visiting the Dominican Republic with five other people during spring break from classes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department layoffs gut its civil rights office, leaving discrimination cases in limbo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department’s civil rights branch is losing nearly half its staff in the Trump administration’s layoffs, effectively gutting an office that already faced a backlog of thousands of complaints from students and families across the nation.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge appears skeptical probationary firings were for performance
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge repeatedly sounded skeptical on Wednesday that the Trump administration’s mass firings of probationary federal workers were made by the government because the employees couldn’t do their jobs, saying the terminations appeared to be part of a larger goal. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A former Tennessee lawmaker wasn't allowed to reverse his guilty plea. Now, Trump has pardoned him
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — President Donald Trump has pardoned a former Republican Tennessee lawmaker who was two weeks into a 21-month prison sentence for an illegal campaign finance scheme that he pleaded guilty to in 2022, before he tried unsuccessfully to take back his plea. Former Sen.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing American in Dominican Republic: Unpredictable ocean currents, beachfront pose major search challenge
item.description: The Dominican Republic beach where UPitt student Sudkisha Konanki vanished last week is known for unpredictable currents and is located near the end of a protective coral reef.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: Those who are over the age of 14 will also need to apply for fingerprinting with authorities, according to the mandate uploaded Wednesday to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Washington sues county for illegally helping ICE
item.description: The state's Keep Washington Working Act restricts the extent local police can assist federal immigration agents.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: 20 charged with murder after 3 die inside Nevada prison
item.description: Charges against the 20 defendants include first-degree murder and attempted murder with gang enhancements, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: T-Mobile customers to get payments up to $25K next month after data breach: Here's who qualifies
item.description: The $350 million T-Mobile agreed to pay in a 2022 class action settlement will finally start going out to customers next month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: California Gov Newsom sets Menendez brothers parole board hearing date in bid for clemency
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced on his podcast Tuesday that Erik and Lyle Menendez will have a parole board hearing on June 13 as they push for clemency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panera unveils green bread bowl for St. Patrick’s Day in select cities
item.description: Panera Bread is turning one of its signature items green in honor of St. Patrick’s Day. The fast-casual chain will launch its limited-edition Pot of Gold, a green sourdough bread bowl filled with its signature Mac and Cheese, on March 16 and 17.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Passenger attacked flight attendant, swallowed rosary beads on plane to Miami: court docs
item.description: His sister told the FBI agent that they were traveling to Haiti “to flee religious attacks of a spiritual nature.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Schools use AI to monitor kids, hoping to prevent violence. Our investigation found security risks
item.description: One student asked a search engine, “Why does my boyfriend hit me?” Another threatened suicide in an email to an unrequited love. A gay teen opened up in an online diary about struggles with homophobic parents, writing they just wanted to be themselves.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE BRYAN of The Seattle Times and SHARON LURYE of The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain 'heavy metals' and pesticides
item.description: A new federal lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain heavy metals, but the organization assures the cookies are safe.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: How microplastics may be stoking antibiotic resistance
item.description: The microscopic shards of plastic found in every corner of the planet may be exacerbating antibiotic resistance, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, KRON4

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works; Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Trump considers easing safety screenings for chemicals
item.description: The Trump administration will consider easing regulations under which it considers the safety of existing chemicals, drawing concerns from public health advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Bags fly free no more: Southwest Airlines to end free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, ABC 4

item.title: Mexico expels alleged US child kidnapper
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – An American who allegedly took his two young children to Mexico without consent and then demanded ransom from the mother is back in Texas to face kidnapping charges.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges against Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy
item.description: Xavier Worthy was arrested Friday and has since been released after the county's district attorney said he would not accept the case at this time.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5
